plan.reasoning: No previous attempts exist, so I must propose the best initial physically sensible plan. The user wants the lowest-energy adsorption configuration for [N]=N on an Ag/Sr alloy surface. Step 0 consistency check: the SMILES is [N]=N, which corresponds to a diazenyl-type species rather than closed-shell N2. The provided atom list shows atom 0 is N, SP2, degree 1, with 1 radical electron; atom 1 is N, SP2, degree 1, with 0 radical electrons. There is no mismatch with a user-described saturated species because none was given. Because I plan to bind through a radical center, I must use adsorbate_type ReactiveSpecies and select only an atom with radical_electrons > 0. Therefore atom index 0 is the valid reactive binding atom; atom 1 is not valid for ReactiveSpecies binding. For early transition-like or electropositive alloy components, N-centered radicals generally prefer strong interaction with the more electropositive metal. Among available ontop sites Ag and Sr, Sr is expected to bind N more strongly than Ag. A radical N species is also most straightforwardly initialized in an end-on geometry using a single N anchor, which matches the ontop site rule requiring exactly one adsorbate binding index. Bridge and hollow could also be interesting, but with a single-attempt cap the most promising initial guess is end-on adsorption of the radical N atom atop Sr. This respects the available site list and uses the correct atom index from the supplied RDKit analysis.
